science.title: Scientists finally solved how H5N1 bird flu hid in dairy cows
science.summary: Researchers uncovered why H5N1 bird flu attacks cows’ udders instead of their lungs: the virus’s preferred receptors are concentrated in mammary tissue. The breakthrough could help scientists predict future bird flu jumps and spot unusual infections before they spread widely.

science.title: After 70 years of excavation, ancient Sardis becomes a UNESCO World Heritage site
science.summary: After nearly seven decades of excavation, the legendary ancient city of Sardis has become a UNESCO World Heritage Site, celebrating years of discoveries that continue to reshape its history. Archaeologists say the biggest breakthroughs don't happen in a single season—they emerge as decades of evidence slowly come toget

science.title: Early humans were bringing fire into caves 1.8 million years ago
science.summary: A new study suggests early humans were using fire in South Africa’s Wonderwerk Cave as far back as 1.79 million years ago. Researchers found burned bones deep inside the cave, where natural wildfires could not have reached, indicating that fire was likely carried in and maintained by human ancestors. The discovery push

science.title: Scientists Turn Ordinary Sunlight Into UV Light in Major Energy Breakthrough
science.summary: Sunlight just got an upgrade: Scientists have developed a material that converts ordinary sunshine into UV light, opening new possibilities for solar-powered technologies. Imagine pouring two cups of warm water together and expecting to get one cup of boiling water. That is impossible in everyday life, but something si

science.title: Scientists discover what triggers belly fat as we age
science.summary: Aging may trigger the appearance of specialized stem cells that supercharge the body's ability to create new belly fat. The discovery reveals a potential biological driver of middle-age weight gain and a promising target for future anti-obesity treatments.

tech.title: The Download: Europe’s heat wave hits the grid, and IBM’s chip targets Moore’s Law
tech.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. tech.title: IBM has unveiled chip technology that could help extend Moore’s Law another decade
tech.summary: IBM has built a new prototype chip with around 100 billion transistors on an area the size of a fingernail, which is twice the density of the company’s previous state-of-the-art technology announced in 2021. The design could pave the way for faster and more energy efficient computers for years to come. For more than ha

tech.title: The Download: introducing the Engineering issue
tech.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Introducing: the Engineering issue We can’t fix everything, but we can be ambitious. We can take on the challenge of making the world better through human ingenuity. That

tech.title: Corgi, the buzzy Y Combinator-backed insurance tech startup, says it didn’t steal an open source product
tech.summary: Corgi became embroiled in controversy when Papermark accused it of stealing its software. Corgi says it did not, raising new questions about vibe coding.

tech.title: Why everyone from OpenAI to SpaceX is building their own chips (and turning up the heat on Nvidia)
tech.summary: Nvidia has dominated the AI chip market for years, but the era of total dependence might be ending.   OpenAI just shared its plans to spice things up with Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in a growing list of companies building their way out of si

medicine.title: Behavioral therapy can be just as effective as medication for Tourette syndrome, says researcher
medicine.summary: Behavioral therapy is just as effective as medication for Tourette syndrome. This is the finding of doctoral research conducted by clinical psychologist Jolande van de Griendt, who will receive her Ph.D. from Radboud University on July 3, 2026. "Medication is still often the first step in treating a tic disorder. But f

medicine.title: Multiple sclerosis impacts daily life far beyond its physical symptoms, new study finds
medicine.summary: Multiple sclerosis (MS) can have a substantial impact on many aspects of life beyond physical health, with 51% of people reporting that the disease affects their social life and 48% reporting that it affects their work, according to new research presented at the European Academy of Neurology (EAN) Congress 2026.

medicine.title: The danger of confusing AI mental health support with therapy
medicine.summary: In a recent episode of British sitcom Amandaland, Anne Flynn turns to ChatGPT for help talking to her teenage son about sex. The episode frames this as "The Chat": the awkward parent-child conversation many adults dread.

medicine.title: How YouTube shapes public understanding of avian influenza
medicine.summary: A new study analyzing more than 11,000 YouTube videos and comments found that online narratives surrounding avian influenza evolved alongside major outbreak developments, including increasing reports of infections in mammals, sporadic human cases and economic consequences. While videos largely reflected scientific and

medicine.title: Hospital AI tool predicts low blood sugar in patients up to 24 hours in advance
medicine.summary: Cedars-Sinai Health Sciences University investigators developed an AI-based model that can identify hospitalized patients at risk of low blood sugar up to 24 hours before the condition occurs. The long short-term memory (LSTM) model, described in npj Digital Medicine, could help clinicians intervene earlier and prevent

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win 2026 Breakthrough, New Horizons prizes
mathematics.summary: Faculty member Shu-Heng Shao, in addition to four MIT alumni, are honored by the Breakthrough Prize Foundation.

mathematics.title: MIT affiliates win AI for Math grants to accelerate mathematical discovery
mathematics.summary: Department of Mathematics researchers David Roe and Andrew Sutherland seek to advance automated theorem proving; four additional MIT alumni also awarded.

mathematics.title: Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones?
mathematics.summary: A decades-old proof showed that seven shuffles are enough to mix up a deck of cards. But it requires you to cut the deck with the precision of a professional magician. A new proof gets around that obstacle. The post Seven Perfect Shuffles Randomize a Deck of Cards. But How Many Sloppy Ones? first appeared on Quanta Mag

mathematics.title: How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math
mathematics.summary: With automated proof-checkers, a problem can be broken up into small chunks, solved bit-by-bit, then reassembled with confidence that every piece is correct. For some, this heralds a new area in mathematical research. The post How Terry Tao Became an Evangelist for AI in Math first appeared on Quanta Magazine

mathematics.title: Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up
mathematics.summary: By replacing the most fundamental concept in topology, Peter Scholze and Dustin Clausen are taking the first step in a far bigger program to understand why numbers behave the way they do. The post Two Researchers Are Rebuilding Mathematics From the Ground Up first appeared on Quanta Magazine

space.title: NASA Tests New Refuel Device for Future In-Space Refueling Missions
space.summary: For NASA’s next generation of deep space exploration missions, spacecraft may need to refuel in Earth orbit before pushing farther into the solar system. Similar to how a gas pump needs a nozzle to fit your fuel tank, future spacecraft could require a special device in order to fill up prior to departure, known as [&#8

space.title: NASA Identifies More Than 40 Space Technologies for Collaboration
space.summary: NASA selected 41 proposals from 37 companies to advance technologies in support of the agency’s goals to establish a long-term presence on the Moon and enable human exploration of Mars. These American companies, picked from NASA’s 2025 Announcement of Collaboration Opportunity (ACO), will mature technologies creating s

space.title: Expedition 73 Crew Reflects on Science, Teamwork, and Life in Orbit
space.summary: On June 16, astronauts and cosmonauts gathered at Space Center Houston to share stories from their missions aboard the International Space Station and recognize the teamwork and people on the ground that made their missions possible.  The Expedition 73 Welcome Home Ceremony brought together members of NASA’s

space.title: A SpaceX rocket will slam into the moon this August. Will we be able to see it?
space.summary: A spent SpaceX Falcon 9 upper stage is set to impact the moon this August, but experts aren't sure if the crash will be visible from Earth.

space.title: Partners, NASA Ready for June Launch of Swift Boost Mission
space.summary: A mission to raise the orbit of NASA’s Swift observatory is poised for launch June 30.

astronomy.title: Exploding Stars Sprinkled Ancient Earth With Radioactive Iron and Plutonium
astronomy.summary: Exploding stars have left their radioactive mark on our planet. Now scientists have found more evidence of a particularly violent event in Earth’s more ancient past. The post Exploding Stars Sprinkled Ancient Earth With Radioactive Iron and Plutonium appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: “Shadow Blaster” Galaxy Might Have Sent High-Energy Neutrino to Earth
astronomy.summary: A star-forming galaxy in the early universe might have sent a ghostly particle known as a neutrino crashing into the ice at Earth’s South Pole, after an 11 billion-year journey through space. The post “Shadow Blaster” Galaxy Might Have Sent High-Energy Neutrino to Earth appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: The Galaxy Living Too Fast
astronomy.summary: Twelve million light years away, a galaxy is living fast and burning bright, forging new stars ten times quicker than our own Milky Way in a frenzy that cannot possibly last. Now the James Webb Space Telescope has cut clean through its veil of dust to count an astonishing 16.5 million of its stars, one by one. So what

astronomy.title: Into the Void: Investigating the Heart of a Giant Elliptical Galaxy
astronomy.summary: The core of the brightest galaxy in the cluster Abell 402 contains a curious void. New observations suggest that an ultra-massive black hole could have excavated this feature. The post Into the Void: Investigating the Heart of a Giant Elliptical Galaxy appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

astronomy.title: Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS Has Cold, Ancient Origins
astronomy.summary: The most recent interstellar visitor was crisscrossing our galaxy for some 10 to 12 billion years before it came near the Sun. The post Interstellar Comet 3I/ATLAS Has Cold, Ancient Origins appeared first on Sky & Telescope.

architecture.title: BIG designs "new kind of urban university" for Bentonville
architecture.summary: Architecture studio BIG has designed a series of buildings for a university being established by the members of the Walton family in Bentonville, Arkansas, USA with three structures that expand on the local vernacular. Members of the Walton family, who are best known as founders of retail giant Walmart, announced that

architecture.title: Island Crest / ONOMA Architecture
architecture.summary: The story of Island Crest is rooted in commitment, preservation, and the decision to thoughtfully re-envision good bones. When the new owners discovered a 1958 home on Mercer Island, they were immediately drawn to its history and character. The couple fell for the building's midcentury design, Pacific Northwest craftsm

architecture.title: Rewilding the City: 6 Unbuilt Projects from the ArchDaily Community
architecture.summary: In the current context of rapid urban environmental changes, such as heatwaves and droughts, new priorities are emerging in the design of public spaces. "Rewilding" refers to the practice of restoring self-sustaining ecosystems through the reintroduction of biodiversity, implementing strategies to reverse the effects o

architecture.title: Snøhetta Reimagines Aino and Alvar Aalto's Paimio Sanatorium as a Wellness and Cultural Destination
architecture.summary: Aino and Alvar Aalto's Paimio Sanatorium is a recognized example of modern architecture for healing, representing a patient-centered approach to hygienism that treated the building itself as a medical instrument. Built between 1929 and 1933, it was designed as a nature-oriented tuberculosis sanatorium, later used as a

architecture.title: Representation as Argument: Lyndon Neri on What Juries Look for in Architecture Competitions
architecture.summary: In an industry defined by building codes, climate urgency, and the pressures of the real estate market, the architectural competition has quietly become one of the discipline's most generative spaces. Unburdened by budgets, clients, or city regulations, competition entries allow architects to think at the edge of what

construction.title: Balfour Beatty hires new legal chief
construction.summary: Ed Egerton, who brings more than 20 years of experience in infrastructure, engineering and energy, is the firm’s second C-suite addition to come from AtkinsRéalis in the last year.

construction.title: DOT earmarks $1.86B for infrastructure damaged by storms
construction.summary: More than $908 million will go toward Hurricane Helene recovery to help states repair roads, bridges, water systems and rail lines.

construction.title: Letter to the Editor: There's Nothing New Under the Sun for Bridges
construction.summary: I get a bit amused by articles on “new” construction methods for bridges, such as the following one in Ohio.

construction.title: Correction: Wrong Casino Collapse Photo
construction.summary: Related Links: Engineering News Record Architectural Record ﻿The story "OSHA Points to Process Errors in Cincinnati Casino Collapse" contained errors. construction.title: Gilbane-Turner JV hands off $2.1B NFL stadium to Buffalo Bills
construction.summary: About 6,000 craft workers clocked nearly 5 million hours over the roughly 60,000-seat venue’s three-year construction timeline. Now it’s gameday ready.

ai.title: Beyond LLMs: Why Scalable Enterprise AI Adoption Depends on Agent Logic
A startup claims it broke through a bottleneck that’s holding back LLMs
ai.summary: The Miami-based AI startup Subquadratic came out of stealth mode last month with a huge claim. It announced that it had solved a mathematical bottleneck that had been holding back large language models for almost a decade. The details were thin, and many people were unconvinced. ai.title: Previewing GPT-5.6 Sol: a next-generation model
ai.summary: OpenAI previews GPT-5.6 Sol, a next-generation model with stronger capabilities in coding, science, and cybersecurity, paired with its most advanced safety stack.

ai.title: How agents are transforming work
ai.summary: A new OpenAI research paper shows how AI agents are transforming work, enabling longer, more complex tasks and expanding productivity across roles.

ai.title: OpenAI and Broadcom unveil LLM-optimized inference chip
ai.summary: OpenAI and Broadcom introduce Jalapeño, a custom AI chip built for LLM inference to improve performance, efficiency, and scale across AI systems.
